items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Halton Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cheshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 11th-13th century
*Coords:* 53.3331, -2.6957
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halton_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Halton%20Castle/@53.3333,-2.6833,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d9/Halton_Castle_%285%29.JPG/330px-Halton_Castle_%285%29.JPG
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* no google places. 

*Description:*
Halton Castle is a castle in the village of Halton, part of the town of Runcorn, Cheshire, England. The castle is on the top of Halton Hill, a sandstone prominence overlooking the village. The original building, a motte-and-bailey castle began in 1071, was replaced with the current sandstone castle in the 13th century. Building alterations continued until at least 1609, when the structure is recorded as in disrepair. The castle is recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building, and a scheduled monument.

_Listing 1 of 10_

*Name:* Lyme Park
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cheshire
*Type:* palace
*Period:* 18th century
*Coords:* 53.3381, -2.0548
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lyme_Park
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Lyme%20Park/@53.35,-2.05,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/South_facade_of_Lyme_Park_house%2C_2013.jpg/330px-South_facade_of_Lyme_Park_house%2C_2013.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* unknown
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Lyme Park is a large estate south of Disley, Cheshire, England. It is managed by the National Trust and consists of a mansion house surrounded by formal gardens and a deer park in the Peak District National Park. The house is the largest in Cheshire and is recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building.

_Listing 2 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Norton Priory
*Country:* england
*Region:* northern england
*County:* Cheshire
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th-16th century
*Coords:* 53.3423, -2.6799
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norton_Priory
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Norton%20Priory/@53.35,-2.5667,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Norton_Priory.jpg/330px-Norton_Priory.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.nortonpriory.org/

*Description:*
Norton Priory is a historic site in Norton, Runcorn, Cheshire, England, comprising the remains of an abbey complex dating from the 12th to 16th centuries, and an 18th-century country house; it is now a museum. The remains are a scheduled ancient monument and are recorded in the National Heritage List for England as a designated Grade I listed building. They are considered to be the most important monastic remains in Cheshire.

_Listing 3 of 10_
